environmental_engineer.Water Treatment: Water treatment provides safe drinking water through sedimentation, filtration, disinfection (chlorine, UV, ozone). Remove contaminants: turbidity, microorganisms, chemicals. Understand regulations (Safe Drinking Water Act).
environmental_engineer.Wastewater Treatment: Wastewater treatment removes pollutants before discharge. Primary (solids removal), secondary (biological treatment), tertiary (advanced treatment). Meet discharge permits (NPDES).
environmental_engineer.Air Quality: Air quality engineering controls air pollution from industrial sources and vehicles. Pollutants: particulate matter, SOx, NOx, VOCs, CO. Technologies: scrubbers, filters, catalytic converters.
environmental_engineer.Solid Waste Management: Solid waste management handles municipal waste through collection, recycling, landfills, incineration, composting. Reduce, reuse, recycle hierarchy. Landfill design prevents groundwater contamination.
environmental_engineer.Remediation: Environmental remediation cleans contaminated sites (soil, groundwater). Technologies: excavation, pump-and-treat, bioremediation, soil vapor extraction. Characterize contamination, assess risks, select remedies.
environmental_engineer.Sustainability: Environmental engineers promote sustainability through life cycle assessment, green design, renewable energy, pollution prevention, and resource conservation. Balance environmental, economic, and social factors.

industrial_engineer.Process Improvement: Industrial engineers optimize processes using lean manufacturing, Six Sigma, time studies, and value stream mapping. Eliminate waste, reduce variation, improve flow and quality.
industrial_engineer.Facility Layout: Facility layout arranges equipment and workstations to minimize material handling, reduce cycle time, and improve flow. Types: product layout (assembly line), process layout (job shop), cellular layout.
industrial_engineer.Ergonomics: Ergonomics designs work environments fitting human capabilities and limitations. Reduce injuries, fatigue, and errors. Consider posture, force, repetition, and environmental factors.
industrial_engineer.Supply Chain Optimization: Supply chain optimization improves flow of materials from suppliers to customers. Inventory optimization, demand forecasting, logistics, supplier management. Use simulation and optimization tools.
industrial_engineer.Quality Management: Quality management ensures products meet specifications through quality planning, control (inspection, SPC), and improvement (PDCA, Six Sigma). Prevent defects rather than detect.
industrial_engineer.Simulation: Simulation models complex systems (manufacturing, logistics, services) to evaluate performance and test improvements. Discrete event simulation widely used. Validate model with real data.

network_engineer.Network Architecture: Network architecture designs network structure: topology (star, mesh, bus), layers (access, distribution, core), redundancy, and scalability. Understand OSI and TCP/IP models.
network_engineer.Routing and Switching: Routing directs packets between networks using routers and protocols (OSPF, BGP). Switching connects devices within networks using switches and VLANs. Configure devices for optimal traffic flow.
network_engineer.Network Security: Network security protects against threats through firewalls, VPNs, IDS/IPS, access controls, and encryption. Defense in depth uses multiple security layers. Monitor for intrusions.
network_engineer.Wireless Networks: Wireless networks use Wi-Fi (802.11) standards. Consider coverage, capacity, security (WPA2/3), and interference. Site surveys optimize access point placement.
network_engineer.Network Troubleshooting: Network troubleshooting diagnoses issues using tools (ping, traceroute, packet analyzers) and systematic approach: identify problem, gather information, isolate cause, implement solution, verify.
network_engineer.Cloud Networking: Cloud networking extends traditional networking to cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP). Virtual networks, load balancing, VPN connectivity, security groups, and multi-cloud integration.

cybersecurity_analyst.Threat Analysis: Threat analysis identifies potential attacks, assesses likelihood and impact, and prioritizes defenses. Understand threat actors, attack vectors, and common vulnerabilities. Stay current on emerging threats.
cybersecurity_analyst.Penetration Testing: Penetration testing simulates attacks to find vulnerabilities before attackers do. Reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, post-exploitation. Automated tools and manual testing. Report findings clearly.
cybersecurity_analyst.Incident Response: Incident response handles security breaches: preparation (plans, tools), detection (monitoring, alerts), containment (isolate), eradication (remove threat), recovery (restore), lessons learned.
cybersecurity_analyst.Security Monitoring: Security monitoring detects threats through SIEM systems collecting logs from firewalls, servers, and endpoints. Write detection rules, investigate alerts, and distinguish true positives from false positives.
cybersecurity_analyst.Vulnerability Management: Vulnerability management identifies, assesses, prioritizes, and remediates vulnerabilities. Regular scanning, patch management, configuration hardening. Risk-based prioritization addresses critical issues first.
cybersecurity_analyst.Security Policies: Security policies define rules and procedures: acceptable use, access control, incident response, data handling. Policies backed by technical controls and user training.

school_counselor.Student Development: School counselors support academic, career, and social-emotional development. Implement comprehensive school counseling program aligned with ASCA standards. Promote equity and access for all students.
school_counselor.Crisis Intervention: Crisis intervention provides immediate support during emergencies (suicide risk, violence, trauma). Assess safety, provide support, connect to resources, follow up. Understand district protocols.
school_counselor.College Counseling: College counseling guides students through college search, applications, financial aid, and transition. Understand admissions, test prep, scholarships. Help students find good fits.
school_counselor.Group Counseling: Group counseling addresses common issues (social skills, grief, divorce, academic skills). Screen members, establish norms, facilitate discussions, and evaluate outcomes.
school_counselor.Data-Driven Counseling: School counselors use data to identify needs, plan interventions, monitor progress, and demonstrate outcomes. Analyze attendance, behavior, achievement data. Close achievement gaps.

social_worker.Case Management: Case managers coordinate services for clients: assessment, care planning, referrals, monitoring, and advocacy. Connect clients to resources (housing, healthcare, employment). Document thoroughly.
social_worker.Crisis Intervention: Social workers respond to crises (abuse, homelessness, mental health emergencies) through immediate support, safety assessment, and connection to resources. Trauma-informed approach.
social_worker.Child Welfare: Child welfare social workers protect children from abuse and neglect. Investigate reports, assess safety, provide services to families, arrange foster care or adoption when necessary.
social_worker.Mental Health Counseling: Social workers provide therapy: individual, family, group. Evidence-based approaches: cognitive-behavioral therapy, motivational interviewing, solution-focused brief therapy.
social_worker.Community Resources: Social workers know community resources: housing assistance, food banks, healthcare, job training, legal aid. Build relationships with agencies. Advocate for policy changes addressing root causes.

special_education_teacher.IEP Development: Individualized Education Programs specify goals, services, accommodations, and modifications for students with disabilities. Collaborate with team (parents, teachers, specialists). Measurable goals, data-driven decisions.
special_education_teacher.Differentiation: Differentiate instruction to meet diverse needs through varying content, process, product, or environment. Use flexible grouping, tiered assignments, and learning centers.
special_education_teacher.Behavioral Interventions: Functional Behavior Assessment (FBA) identifies function of behavior. Behavior Intervention Plan (BIP) teaches replacement behaviors. Positive reinforcement, teaching strategies, environmental modifications.
special_education_teacher.Assistive Technology: Assistive technology supports students with disabilities: text-to-speech, speech-to-text, communication devices, adapted keyboards. Match technology to student needs. Train use.
special_education_teacher.Inclusion Strategies: Inclusion educates students with disabilities in general education with appropriate supports. Co-teaching, accommodations, modifications, collaboration. Least restrictive environment principle.

librarian.Information Literacy: Information literacy teaches finding, evaluating, and using information effectively. Evaluate sources for authority, accuracy, bias. Understand academic vs popular sources. Cite properly to avoid plagiarism.
librarian.Collection Development: Collection development builds library collections aligned with user needs. Selection criteria: relevance, quality, diversity. Balance print and digital. Weed outdated materials.
librarian.Research Assistance: Librarians help users find information through reference interviews, database searching, and instruction. Understand user needs, suggest resources, teach search strategies.
librarian.Library Technology: Library technology includes integrated library systems (cataloging, circulation), databases, digital collections, and makerspaces. Stay current on emerging technologies serving users.
librarian.Programming: Library programming engages communities through story times, book clubs, workshops, author visits, and summer reading. Promote literacy and lifelong learning.

academic_advisor.Degree Planning: Academic advisors help students plan course sequences meeting degree requirements. Understand curricula, prerequisites, and graduation requirements. Create four-year plans.
academic_advisor.Student Success: Advising supports student success through goal-setting, time management, study skills, campus resources, and addressing barriers. Intrusive advising reaches out proactively.
academic_advisor.Career Exploration: Advisors help students explore majors and careers through assessments, informational interviews, and job shadowing. Connect academic choices to career goals.
academic_advisor.Policy Knowledge: Advisors understand institutional policies: registration, financial aid, academic standing, degree requirements. Guide students through processes correctly.
academic_advisor.Crisis Support: Advisors recognize students in crisis (mental health, financial, academic) and refer to appropriate resources (counseling, financial aid, tutoring). Follow up on referrals.
